marketState: neutral, tilted cautious — price already running above fair value with short-term technicals overbought
summary: Seasonality is only mildly bullish (56/50/56/63% odds of a higher price over 1-4 weeks, price +3.3pp above its median seasonal path) and explicitly flagged 'Low' reliability, so it shouldn't carry a trade on its own. Weather demand is well above the 5-year normal in the next 7 days (+11.1%, rangePos 0.87 near the top of its range) but eases in the 8-16 day window (+6.9%, rangePos 0.63) and has ticked down 7.4 units in the last 24h, sitting near the bottom of its own weekly range - a fading, not building, tailwind. Front-month price ($3.245) is 'SLIGHTLY OVER' modeled fair value at every horizon out to 21 days, and notably already exceeds even the plus-21-day fair estimate ($3.066), meaning the rally has front-run its own fundamental curve rather than being validated by it. Storage is a genuine bearish headwind (+175 Bcf surplus vs 5y, plus a +13 Bcf bearish build surprise), while COT managed-money sits at an 18th-percentile net-short extreme, a contrarian-bullish positioning offset. Near-term technicals are overbought (5m/15m RSI 74-84, price above the upper Bollinger band) even as 4h/1d look closer to neutral, favoring mean reversion over continuation in the next 1-10 days.

longTerm.rationale: Mildly bullish seasonality/weather and a contrarian-bullish COT extreme (managed-money net short at the 18th 5y percentile) are offset by a confirmed storage surplus (+175 Bcf vs 5y, bearish build surprise) and a price that already trades above its own plus-21D fair-value estimate. With seasonality reliability explicitly flagged 'Low' this week and the weather tailwind fading by the 8-16D window, the top-priority evidence is too mixed to size a directional multi-week position.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a pullback into the $2.75-2.95 fair-value zone with weather demand still above-normal to build a LONG, or (b) a confirmed break below ~$3.00 alongside continued/growing storage surpluses to justify a SHORT.

shortTerm.rationale: Front trades 'SLIGHTLY OVER' fair value at every modeled horizon while 5m/15m/1h are overbought (RSI 74-84, price above the upper Bollinger band), favoring a mean-reversion fade toward the fair-value band ($3.06 plus7D/14D fair, $2.93 now-fair) rather than chasing the extension. This is a tactical, technically-driven fade, not a fundamentals call.
shortTerm.invalidation: A push and hold above the $3.43 fair-value-now band ceiling, or a fresh spike in 1-7D weather demand/rangePos, would invalidate the fade thesis.

keyDrivers: Front price already above modeled fair value at every horizon (now through +21D), including the model's own plus-21D estimate
1-7D weather demand +11.1% vs 5y (rangePos 0.87) but fading into the 8-16D window (+6.9%, rangePos 0.63) and down 7.4 in the last 24h
Storage surplus +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish build surprise (+13 Bcf vs forecast)
Managed-money COT net short at an 18th-percentile extreme (contrarian bullish offset)
Short-frame technicals overbought (RSI 74-84, above upper Bollinger) vs more neutral 4h/1d
risks: Seasonality signal flagged 'Low' reliability (51pp 10-90% spread) - don't over-weight the seasonal tilt
A resurgence in near-term heat/cooling demand would quickly invalidate the short-term fade
